uplink.description: Full reading: battery, temperature, humidity, wind direction, pressure, wind speed, rainfall
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload built from the WTS506 channel/type TLV wire format; expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js (the oracle) and mapping its raw keys into the normalized vocabulary (temperature/humidity/pressure -> air, wind_direction/wind_speed -> wind, rainfall_total -> rain.cumulative, battery % -> batteryPercent, rainfall_counter -> rainfallCounter).

uplink.description: Edge values: negative temperature, full humidity, low-end pressure, zero wind/rain
uplink._note: Synthetic edge-case payload (int16 two's-complement negative temperature 0xFFD6 = -4.2 °C, humidity 200/2 = 100 %, pressure 9450/10 = 945 hPa within the 900-1100 atmospheric bound, zero wind and rainfall). expected.data derived from reference/upstream-codec.js (oracle) mapped into the normalized vocabulary.

uplink.description: Minimal weather-station reading: temperature + barometric pressure only
uplink._note: Synthetic payload carrying only the two weather-station required channels (temperature 358/10 = 35.8 °C, pressure 10955/10 = 1095.5 hPa near the upper 1100 atmospheric bound). expected.data derived from reference/upstream-codec.js (oracle) mapped into air.temperature and air.pressure.

Synthetic error payload: channel/type pair 0xFF/0xFF matches no WTS506 channel, so decoding recognizes nothing and returns an error (the upstream oracle returns an empty {} for the same input; our codec surfaces it as an explicit error per the output contract).
